name: Taittiriya Upanishad
summary: The Taittiriya Upanishad has three sections: Shiksha Valli (on phonetics and ethics), Brahmananda Valli (on the five sheaths and Brahman as bliss), and Bhrigu Valli (Bhrigu's progressive discovery of Brahman). It famously declares: satyam jnanam anantam brahma — Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinity.

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म | यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन् |
सोऽश्नुते सर्वान् कामान् सह | ब्रह्मणा विपश्चितेति ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration:
satyaṃ jñānam anantaṃ brahma | yo veda nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ parame vyoman |
so'śnute sarvān kāmān saha | brahmaṇā vipaściteti ||2.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: hidden in the cave (of the heart)

verses.english_translation: Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinity. One who knows it hidden in the cave of the heart, in the highest ether, attains all desires together with the all-knowing Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सत्यम् — परिणामरहितम्। ज्ञानम् — चैतन्यस्वरूपम्। अनन्तम् — देशकालवस्तुपरिच्छेदरहितम्। एतत् ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Satyam — that which does not undergo transformation. Jnanam — whose very nature is consciousness. Anantam — unlimited by space, time, or object. This is Brahman. One who knows this hidden in the heart attains all.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नमयो ह्येवायं पुरुषः | तस्येदमेव शिरः | अयं दक्षिणः पक्षः | अयमुत्तरः पक्षः | अयमात्मा | इदं पुच्छं प्रतिष्ठा ||२.१.२||
verses.transliteration: annamayo hy evāyaṃ puruṣaḥ | tasyedam eva śiraḥ | ayaṃ dakṣiṇaḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam uttaraḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam ātmā | idaṃ pucchaṃ pratiṣṭhā ||2.1.2||
verses.english_translation: This person is indeed made of the essence of food. This is his head, this his right wing, this his left wing, this his trunk, this his tail — the foundation.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नमयः — अन्नरसपरिणामः स्थूलशरीरम्। पञ्चकोशानां मध्ये बाह्यतमः कोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The annamaya kosha (food sheath) is the gross body, a transformation of the essence of food. It is the outermost of the five sheaths. The teaching progressively moves inward to discover the true Self beyond all sheaths.

verses.sanskrit:
आनन्दो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | आनन्दाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते |
आनन्देन जातानि जीवन्ति | आनन्दं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.६.१||
verses.transliteration:
ānando brahmeti vyajānāt | ānandād dhy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante |
ānandena jātāni jīvanti | ānandaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.6.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: Bliss is Brahman. For from bliss alone are all these beings born; by bliss they live once born; into bliss they return at death.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आनन्दः — परमानन्दः — ब्रह्मैव। सर्वाणि भूतानि आनन्दात् जायन्ते — ब्रह्मण एव उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bliss is Brahman itself. All beings are born from Brahman-bliss, sustained by it, and return to it. This is Bhrigu's final realization after progressive inquiry — the ultimate ground is pure bliss (ananda).

verses.sanskrit: ॐ शं नो मित्रः शं वरुणः | शं नो भवत्वर्यमा | शं न इन्द्रो बृहस्पतिः | शं नो विष्णुरुरुक्रमः | नमो ब्रह्मणे | नमस्ते वायो | त्वमेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्मासि | त्वामेव प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्म वदिष्यामि | ऋतं वदिष्यामि | सत्यं वदिष्यामि | तन्मामवतु | तद्वक्तारमवतु | अवतु माम् | अवतु वक्तारम् ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: oṃ śaṃ no mitraḥ śaṃ varuṇaḥ | śaṃ no bhavatv aryamā | śaṃ na indro bṛhaspatiḥ | śaṃ no viṣṇur urukramaḥ | namo brahmaṇe | namas te vāyo | tvam eva pratyakṣaṃ brahmāsi | tvām eva pratyakṣaṃ brahma vadiṣyāmi | ṛtaṃ vadiṣyāmi | satyaṃ vadiṣyāmi | tan mām avatu | tad vaktāram avatu | avatu mām | avatu vaktāram ||1.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: you are the directly perceived Brahman
I shall speak the truth (cosmic order)

verses.english_translation: Om. May Mitra be auspicious to us; may Varuna be auspicious; may Aryaman be auspicious; may Indra and Brihaspati be auspicious; may Vishnu of wide strides be auspicious. Salutation to Brahman. Salutation to you, O Vayu — you are indeed the directly perceived Brahman. I shall declare you as the directly perceived Brahman. I shall speak the cosmic truth. I shall speak personal truth. May that protect me. May that protect the teacher. May it protect me. May it protect the teacher.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: शं नः मित्रः — मित्रादयः देवाः अस्माकम् शुभकराः भवन्तु। त्वम् प्रत्यक्षम् ब्रह्म — वायुः प्रत्यक्षः — ब्रह्मणः प्रतिनिधिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This opening prayer invokes all the cosmic deities for auspiciousness. Vayu (wind/prana) is called the 'directly perceived Brahman' because among all cosmic forces, prana is most immediately experienced. The student vows to speak both cosmic truth (rita) and personal truth (satya).

verses.sanskrit: शीक्षां व्याख्यास्यामः | वर्णः स्वरः | मात्रा बलम् | साम सन्तानः | इत्युक्तः शीक्षाध्यायः ||१.२.१||
verses.transliteration: śīkṣāṃ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ | varṇaḥ svaraḥ | mātrā balam | sāma santānaḥ | ity uktaḥ śīkṣādhyāyaḥ ||1.2.1||
verses.english_translation: We shall explain phonetics: sound, accent, quantity, force, evenness, and continuity. Thus is stated the chapter on phonetics.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: शीक्षा — वेदाङ्गम् — वर्णोच्चारणशास्त्रम्। वर्णः — अक्षरम्। स्वरः — उदात्तादिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Shiksha (phonetics) is the first of the six Vedic auxiliaries. It teaches the correct pronunciation of Vedic sounds. The six elements — sound, accent, quantity, force, evenness, and continuity — ensure the Vedic mantras are chanted correctly for their full effect.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यं वद | धर्मं चर | स्वाध्यायान्मा प्रमदः | आचार्याय प्रियं धनमाहृत्य प्रजातन्तुं मा व्यवच्छेत्सीः ||१.११.१||
verses.transliteration: satyaṃ vada | dharmaṃ cara | svādhyāyān mā pramadaḥ | ācāryāya priyaṃ dhanam āhṛtya prajātantuṃ mā vyavacchetsīḥ ||1.11.1||

word_meanings.meaning: bring pleasing wealth to the teacher

verses.english_translation: Speak truth. Practice dharma. Do not neglect self-study. Having brought pleasing wealth to the teacher, do not cut the thread of progeny.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सत्यम् वद — सत्यम् एव वद — असत्यम् मा वद। धर्मम् चर — धर्माचरणम् कुरु। स्वाध्यायात् मा प्रमदः — वेदाध्ययनम् मा त्यज।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This is the teacher's farewell instruction to the graduating student. Speak truth, practice dharma, continue Vedic study, honor the teacher with gifts, and continue the tradition. These are the foundational ethical instructions of the Taittiriya Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मादन्नरसमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा प्राणमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.२.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād annarasamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā prāṇamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.2.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of food-essence is another inner Self made of prana. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नरसमयात् — स्थूलशरीरात् — अन्यः — भिन्नः — प्राणमयः आत्मा — प्राणकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the gross body (annamaya kosha) is a subtler body made of prana (pranamaya kosha). The prana-body fills and animates the food-body. This is the second of the five sheaths, progressively subtler as we move inward toward the true Self.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मात्प्राणमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा मनोमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.३.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmāt prāṇamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā manomayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.3.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of prana is another inner Self made of mind. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणमयात् अन्यः — प्राणकोशात् सूक्ष्मतरः — मनोमयः — मनःकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the prana-body is the mind-body (manomaya kosha). The mind-body is subtler and fills the prana-body. The mind includes the five sense organs and the mind (manas). This is the third sheath.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मान्मनोमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा विज्ञानमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.४.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmān manomayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā vijñānamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.4.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of mind is another inner Self made of intellect. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयात् अन्यः — मनःकोशात् सूक्ष्मतरः — विज्ञानमयः — बुद्धिकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the mind-body is the intellect-body (vijnanamaya kosha). The intellect (buddhi) is the faculty of discrimination and decision. It is subtler than the mind and is the fourth sheath.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्माद्विज्ञानमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्माऽऽनन्दमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.५.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād vijñānamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmānandamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.5.1||
verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of intellect is another inner Self made of bliss. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: विज्ञानमयात् अन्यः — बुद्धिकोशात् सूक्ष्मतरः — आनन्दमयः — आनन्दकोशः — कारणशरीरम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The innermost sheath is the bliss-body (anandamaya kosha), which corresponds to deep sleep. It is the causal body. Beyond even this is the true Self — pure consciousness — which is not a sheath at all but the witness of all five sheaths.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं न निन्द्यात् | तद्व्रतम् | प्राणो वा अन्नम् | शरीरमन्नादम् | प्राणे शरीरं प्रतिष्ठितम् | शरीरे प्राणः प्रतिष्ठितः ||३.७.१||
verses.transliteration: annaṃ na nindyāt | tad vratam | prāṇo vā annam | śarīram annādam | prāṇe śarīraṃ pratiṣṭhitam | śarīre prāṇaḥ pratiṣṭhitaḥ ||3.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: the body is the eater of food
the body is established in prana

verses.english_translation: One should not despise food — that is the vow. Prana is indeed food; the body is the eater of food. The body is established in prana; prana is established in the body.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नम् न निन्द्यात् — अन्नम् ब्रह्मरूपम् — तस्य निन्दा न कार्या। प्राणः अन्नम् — प्राणः अन्नेन जीवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Food should not be despised because food is Brahman in manifest form. The entire teaching of the Taittiriya shows that Brahman manifests as food, prana, mind, intellect, and bliss. To despise food is to despise Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: यदो ह्येवैष एतस्मिन्नदृश्येऽनात्म्येऽनिरुक्तेऽनिलयनेऽभयं प्रतिष्ठां विन्दते | अथ सोऽभयं गतो भवति ||२.७.१||
verses.transliteration: yado hy evaiṣa etasminn adṛśye'nātmye'nirukte'nilayane'bhayaṃ pratiṣṭhāṃ vindate | atha so'bhayaṃ gato bhavati ||2.7.1||
verses.english_translation: When one finds fearless support in that which is unseen, bodiless, unutterable, and supportless — then one has gone to fearlessness.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अदृश्ये — इन्द्रियागोचरे। अनात्म्ये — शरीररहिते। अनिरुक्ते — वाचामगोचरे। अभयम् प्रतिष्ठाम् विन्दते — ब्रह्मणि आश्रयम् लभते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: When the seeker finds support in Brahman — which is beyond the senses, bodiless, beyond speech, and self-supporting — then all fear vanishes. Fear arises from duality; in the non-dual Brahman, there is no 'other' to fear.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखम् | नाल्पे सुखमस्ति | भूमैव सुखम् | भूमा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्यः ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai bhūmā tat sukham | nālpe sukham asti | bhūmaiva sukham | bhūmā tv eva vijijñāsitavyaḥ ||2.8.1||

word_meanings.meaning: there is no bliss in the finite

verses.english_translation: The infinite is bliss. There is no bliss in the finite. The infinite alone is bliss. The infinite alone should be inquired into.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: भूमा — अनन्तम् — तत् सुखम्। अल्पे — परिच्छिन्ने — सुखम् नास्ति। भूमा — ब्रह्म — एव सुखम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The infinite (Brahman) is bliss. Finite things cannot give true bliss — only temporary pleasure. The infinite Brahman alone is the source of all bliss. Therefore Brahman should be inquired into — this is the purpose of the Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: यदा ह्येवैष एतस्मिन्नुदरमन्तरं कुरुते | अथ तस्य भयं भवति ||२.७.१||
verses.transliteration: yadā hy evaiṣa etasminn udaram antaraṃ kurute | atha tasya bhayaṃ bhavati ||2.7.1||
verses.english_translation: When one makes even a slight separation in this (Brahman), then fear arises for him.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतस्मिन् — ब्रह्मणि — उदरम् अन्तरम् कुरुते — भेदम् पश्यति — तस्य भयम् भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: When one sees even a slight separation or difference in Brahman — when one perceives duality — fear arises. Fear is the product of duality. In the non-dual Brahman, there is no 'other' to fear. This is why the Upanishad teaches unity.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | अन्नाद्धि खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | अन्नेन जातानि जीवन्ति | अन्नं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.२.१||
verses.transliteration: annaṃ brahmeti vyajānāt | annād dhi khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | annena jātāni jīvanti | annaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.2.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: food is Brahman. For from food alone are all these beings born; by food they live once born; into food they return at death.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नम् ब्रह्म — अन्नम् ब्रह्मरूपम्। अन्नात् जायन्ते — अन्नरसात् शरीरम् उत्पद्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's first realization is that food is Brahman. All beings are born from food, live by food, and return to food. But this is only the first step — Pippalada sends him back to inquire further, leading to deeper realizations.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | प्राणाद्धि खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | प्राणेन जातानि जीवन्ति | प्राणं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.३.१||
verses.transliteration: prāṇo brahmeti vyajānāt | prāṇād dhi khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | prāṇena jātāni jīvanti | prāṇaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.3.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: prana is Brahman. For from prana alone are all these beings born; by prana they live; into prana they return.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: प्राणः ब्रह्म — प्राणः ब्रह्मरूपः। प्राणात् जायन्ते — प्राणशक्त्या शरीरम् उत्पद्यते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's second realization is that prana is Brahman. Prana is subtler than food — it is the life-force that animates the body. But Varuna sends him back again for deeper inquiry.

verses.sanskrit: मनो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | मनसो ह्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | मनसा जातानि जीवन्ति | मनः प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.४.१||
verses.transliteration: mano brahmeti vyajānāt | manaso hy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | manasā jātāni jīvanti | manaḥ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.4.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: mind is Brahman. For from mind alone are all these beings born; by mind they live; into mind they return.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनः ब्रह्म — मनः ब्रह्मरूपम्। मनसः जायन्ते — मनःसंकल्पात् सृष्टिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's third realization is that mind is Brahman. Mind is subtler than prana — it is the faculty of thought and will. But Varuna sends him back once more for the deepest realization.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञानं ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | विज्ञानाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | विज्ञानेन जातानि जीवन्ति | विज्ञानं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.५.१||
verses.transliteration: vijñānaṃ brahmeti vyajānāt | vijñānād dhy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | vijñānena jātāni jīvanti | vijñānaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.5.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: consciousness is Brahman. For from consciousness alone are all these beings born; by consciousness they live; into consciousness they return.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: विज्ञानम् ब्रह्म — बुद्धिः ब्रह्मरूपा। विज्ञानात् जायन्ते — चैतन्यशक्त्या सृष्टिः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bhrigu's fourth realization is that consciousness (vijnana/intellect) is Brahman. This is deeper than mind. But Varuna sends him back one final time for the ultimate realization — that bliss (ananda) is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: मातृदेवो भव | पितृदेवो भव | आचार्यदेवो भव | अतिथिदेवो भव ||१.११.२||
verses.transliteration: mātṛdevo bhava | pitṛdevo bhava | ācāryadevo bhava | atithidevo bhava ||1.11.2||

word_meanings.meaning: let your mother be your god
let your father be your god
let your teacher be your god
let the guest be your god

verses.english_translation: Let your mother be your god. Let your father be your god. Let your teacher be your god. Let the guest be your god.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मातृदेवः — माता देवः यस्य — सः। पितृदेवः — पिता देवः यस्य। आचार्यदेवः — आचार्यः देवः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teacher's farewell instruction includes these four great ethical teachings. Mother, father, teacher, and guest are to be treated as gods — with reverence and service. This is the foundation of dharmic life in the Vedic tradition.

verses.sanskrit: यान्यनवद्यानि कर्माणि | तानि सेवितव्यानि | नो इतराणि | यान्यस्माकं सुचरितानि | तानि त्वयोपास्यानि ||१.११.३||
verses.transliteration: yāny anavadyāni karmāṇi | tāni sevitavyāni | no itarāṇi | yāny asmākaṃ sucaritāni | tāni tvayopāsyāni ||1.11.3||
verses.english_translation: Blameless actions should be practiced, not others. Whatever good conduct of ours you have seen — that should be followed by you.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अनवद्यानि — निर्दोषाणि — कर्माणि — सेवितव्यानि। सुचरितानि — शोभनाचरणानि — उपास्यानि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teacher instructs the student to practice only blameless actions and to follow the good conduct of the teacher. This is the practical ethical teaching of the Taittiriya — dharmic living is the foundation for spiritual knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्यैवानन्दस्यान्यानि भूतानि मात्रामुपजीवन्ति ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: etasyaivānandasyānyāni bhūtāni mātrām upajīvanti ||2.8.1||
verses.english_translation: All other beings live on a fraction of this very bliss.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतस्य — ब्रह्मानन्दस्य — मात्राम् — अंशम् — अन्यानि भूतानि — उपजीवन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All beings — humans, gods, and all creatures — live on a mere fraction of Brahman's bliss. The bliss we experience in any pleasure is a tiny reflection of the infinite bliss of Brahman. This is why all beings seek happiness — they are seeking Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: मनुष्याणां शतं वर्षाणि | तत्सार्धमायुः | तस्य मनुष्यानन्दः | स एको मनुष्यगन्धर्वाणामानन्दः ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: manuṣyāṇāṃ śataṃ varṣāṇi | tat sārdham āyuḥ | tasya manuṣyānandaḥ | sa eko manuṣyagandharvāṇām ānandaḥ ||2.8.1||

word_meanings.meaning: a hundred years of human life

verses.english_translation: A hundred years of human life — that is the full lifespan. That is human bliss. That is one unit of the bliss of human gandharvas.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनुष्यानन्दः — मनुष्यस्य परमानन्दः — एकः मनुष्यगन्धर्वानन्दः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Taittiriya presents a scale of bliss from human to divine. The maximum human bliss is one unit of the bliss of human gandharvas. This scale continues upward through gods to Brahman, whose bliss is infinite. All lesser bliss is a fraction of Brahman's bliss.

verses.sanskrit: अभयं वै ब्रह्म | अभयं हि वै ब्रह्म भवति | य एवं वेद ||२.९.१||
verses.transliteration: abhayaṃ vai brahma | abhayaṃ hi vai brahma bhavati | ya evaṃ veda ||2.9.1||
verses.english_translation: Brahman is indeed fearlessness. One who knows thus becomes Brahman — becomes fearless.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अभयम् ब्रह्म — ब्रह्म अभयस्वरूपम्। य एवम् वेद — सः अभयम् भवति — ब्रह्म एव भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is fearlessness itself. Fear arises from duality — from the perception of an 'other.' In the non-dual Brahman, there is no other, hence no fear. One who knows Brahman becomes Brahman and becomes fearless.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं न परिचक्षीत | तद्व्रतम् | प्राणो वा अन्नम् | शरीरमन्नादम् ||३.७.१||
verses.transliteration: annaṃ na paricakṣīta | tad vratam | prāṇo vā annam | śarīram annādam ||3.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: the body is the eater of food

verses.english_translation: One should not despise food — that is the vow. Prana is indeed food; the body is the eater of food.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नम् न परिचक्षीत — अन्नम् ब्रह्मरूपम् — तस्य निन्दा न कार्या।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Food should not be despised because food is Brahman in manifest form. The entire teaching of the Taittiriya shows that Brahman manifests as food, prana, mind, intellect, and bliss. To despise food is to despise Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं बहु कुर्वीत | तद्व्रतम् | पृथिवी वा अन्नम् | आकाशोऽन्नादः ||३.८.१||
verses.transliteration: annaṃ bahu kurvīta | tad vratam | pṛthivī vā annam | ākāśo'nnādaḥ ||3.8.1||
verses.english_translation: One should produce much food — that is the vow. Earth is indeed food; space is the eater.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नम् बहु कुर्वीत — अन्नम् उत्पादयेत् — अतिथिभ्यः दद्यात्। पृथिवी — अन्नम् — आकाशः — अन्नादः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One should produce much food and share it generously. Earth is the source of food; space is the container. This ethical teaching — produce and share food — is part of the Taittiriya's practical dharmic instruction.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽकामयत | बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति | स तपोऽतप्यत | स तपस्तप्त्वा | इदं सर्वमसृजत | यदिदं किञ्च | तत्सृष्ट्वा | तदेवानुप्राविशत् ||२.६.१||
verses.transliteration: so'kāmayata | bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti | sa tapo'tapyata | sa tapas taptvā | idaṃ sarvam asṛjata | yad idaṃ kiñca | tat sṛṣṭvā | tad evānuprāviśat ||2.6.1||

word_meanings.meaning: may I become many, may I be born

verses.english_translation: He desired: 'May I become many, may I be born.' He performed austerity. Having performed austerity, he created all this — whatever exists. Having created it, he entered into that very creation.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: बहु स्याम् — बहुधा भवेयम् — इति अकामयत। तपः अतप्यत — संकल्पम् अकरोत्। इदम् सर्वम् असृजत — जगत् उत्पादयत। तत् एव अनुप्राविशत् — जगत् अन्तः प्रविशत्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman desired to become many and performed austerity (tapas = creative will). It created the entire universe and then entered into it as the inner Self of all beings. This is the teaching of immanence — Brahman is both the creator and the creation, both transcendent and immanent.

verses.sanskrit: स एको मानुषानन्दः | ते ये शतं मनुष्यगन्धर्वाणामानन्दाः | स एको मनुष्यगन्धर्वाणामानन्दः ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: sa eko mānuṣānandaḥ | te ye śataṃ manuṣyagandharvāṇām ānandāḥ | sa eko manuṣyagandharvāṇām ānandaḥ ||2.8.1||

word_meanings.meaning: a hundred units of human gandharva bliss
one unit of human gandharva bliss

verses.english_translation: That is one unit of human bliss. A hundred units of human bliss equal one unit of human gandharva bliss.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः मानुषानन्दः — मनुष्यस्य परमानन्दः — एकः मनुष्यगन्धर्वानन्दः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The scale of bliss continues upward. One unit of human bliss equals one unit of human gandharva bliss. This scale continues through divine gandharvas, ancestors, gods, Indra, Brihaspati, Prajapati, and finally Brahman — whose bliss is infinite.

verses.sanskrit: ते ये शतं देवानामानन्दाः | स एको देवानामानन्दः | स एको बृहस्पतेरानन्दः ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: te ye śataṃ devānām ānandāḥ | sa eko devānām ānandaḥ | sa eko bṛhaspater ānandaḥ ||2.8.1||

word_meanings.meaning: a hundred units of divine bliss

verses.english_translation: A hundred units of divine bliss equal one unit of divine bliss. That equals one unit of Brihaspati's bliss.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: देवानाम् आनन्दः — बृहस्पतेः आनन्दः — प्रजापतेः आनन्दः — ब्रह्मानन्दः — क्रमेण वर्धते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The scale of bliss continues upward through the gods to Brihaspati, Prajapati, and finally Brahman. Each level is a hundred times greater than the previous. This shows that all bliss is a fraction of Brahman's infinite bliss.

verses.sanskrit: ते ये शतं प्रजापतेरानन्दाः | स एको ब्रह्मण आनन्दः | स य एषोऽन्तरादित्ये हिरण्मयः पुरुषो दृश्यते ||२.८.१||
verses.transliteration: te ye śataṃ prajāpater ānandāḥ | sa eko brahmaṇa ānandaḥ | sa ya eṣo'ntarāditye hiraṇmayaḥ puruṣo dṛśyate ||2.8.1||

word_meanings.meaning: a hundred units of Prajapati's bliss
the golden Person within the sun

verses.english_translation: A hundred units of Prajapati's bliss equal one unit of Brahman's bliss. That is the golden Person seen within the sun.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मणः आनन्दः — अनन्तः। सर्वे आनन्दाः — ब्रह्मानन्दस्य अंशाः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman's bliss is infinite — all other bliss is a fraction of it. The golden Person within the sun is Brahman in its cosmic form. All beings seek happiness because they are seeking Brahman — the source of all bliss.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽश्नुते सर्वान् कामान् सह | ब्रह्मणा विपश्चितेति ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration: so'śnute sarvān kāmān saha | brahmaṇā vipaściteti ||2.1.1||
verses.english_translation: He attains all desires together with the all-knowing Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वान् कामान् अश्नुते — सह ब्रह्मणा — ब्रह्मैव भूत्वा — सर्वान् कामान् प्राप्नोति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The knower of Brahman attains all desires — not by fulfilling individual desires one by one, but by becoming Brahman itself, which is the fulfillment of all desires. When one becomes the all-knowing Brahman, there is nothing left to desire.

verses.sanskrit: आनन्दो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् | आनन्दाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते | आनन्देन जातानि जीवन्ति | आनन्दं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति ||३.६.१||
verses.transliteration: ānando brahmeti vyajānāt | ānandād dhy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | ānandena jātāni jīvanti | ānandaṃ prayanty abhisaṃviśantīti ||3.6.1||
verses.english_translation: He knew: Bliss is Brahman. For from bliss alone are all these beings born; by bliss they live once born; into bliss they return at death.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आनन्दः — परमानन्दः — ब्रह्मैव। सर्वाणि भूतानि आनन्दात् जायन्ते — ब्रह्मण एव उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Bliss is Brahman itself. All beings are born from Brahman-bliss, sustained by it, and return to it. This is Bhrigu's final realization — the ultimate ground is pure bliss (ananda). This is the culminating teaching of the Taittiriya Upanishad.

verses.sanskrit: अहमन्नमहमन्नमहमन्नम् | अहमन्नादोऽहमन्नादोऽहमन्नादः | अहं श्लोककृदहं श्लोककृदहं श्लोककृत् ||३.१०.६||
verses.transliteration: aham annam aham annam aham annam | aham annādo'ham annādo'ham annādaḥ | ahaṃ ślokakṛd ahaṃ ślokakṛd ahaṃ ślokakṛt ||3.10.6||

word_meanings.meaning: I am the eater of food
I am the maker of verses/glory

verses.english_translation: I am food! I am food! I am food! I am the eater of food! I am the eater of food! I am the eater of food! I am the maker of glory!
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अहम् अन्नम् — अहम् ब्रह्म — अन्नरूपम्। अहम् अन्नादः — अहम् ब्रह्म — अन्नादरूपम्। उभयम् अहम् एव।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This ecstatic declaration of the liberated sage expresses the realization of non-duality. 'I am food' — I am the object. 'I am the eater' — I am the subject. Both subject and object are Brahman. The liberated one sees no distinction between the eater and the eaten — all is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मविदाप्नोति परम् | तदेषाऽभ्युक्ता | सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म | यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन् ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration: brahmavid āpnoti param | tad eṣā'bhyuktā | satyaṃ jñānam anantaṃ brahma | yo veda nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ parame vyoman ||2.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: the knower of Brahman attains the supreme
hidden in the cave of the heart

verses.english_translation: The knower of Brahman attains the supreme. This has been declared: Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinity. One who knows it hidden in the cave of the heart, in the supreme ether, attains all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ब्रह्मवित् — परम् आप्नोति — ब्रह्मैव भवति। सत्यम् ज्ञानम् अनन्तम् — ब्रह्मणः लक्षणम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The knower of Brahman attains the supreme — becomes Brahman itself. The definition of Brahman is given: satyam (truth/existence), jnanam (knowledge/consciousness), anantam (infinity). These three together define Brahman as the infinite, self-luminous, eternal existence.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मादात्मन आकाशः सम्भूतः | आकाशाद्वायुः | वायोरग्निः | अग्नेरापः | अद्भ्यः पृथिवी ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād ātmana ākāśaḥ sambhūtaḥ | ākāśād vāyuḥ | vāyor agniḥ | agner āpaḥ | adbhyaḥ pṛthivī ||2.1.1||
verses.english_translation: From this Self, space arose; from space, air; from air, fire; from fire, water; from water, earth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आत्मनः — ब्रह्मणः — आकाशः — वायुः — अग्निः — आपः — पृथिवी — क्रमेण उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The five elements arise in sequence from Brahman: space, air, fire, water, earth. This is the cosmological sequence of the Taittiriya — from the subtlest (space) to the grossest (earth). All creation is a manifestation of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: पृथिव्या ओषधयः | ओषधीभ्योऽन्नम् | अन्नात्पुरुषः ||२.१.२||
verses.transliteration: pṛthivyā oṣadhayaḥ | oṣadhībhyo'nnam | annāt puruṣaḥ ||2.1.2||
verses.english_translation: From earth, plants; from plants, food; from food, the person.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पृथिव्याः — ओषधयः — अन्नम् — पुरुषः — क्रमेण उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The chain of creation continues: from earth come plants, from plants comes food, from food comes the person (body). The entire physical body is made of food — this is why the Taittiriya calls the body the 'food sheath' (annamaya kosha).

verses.sanskrit: स वा एष पुरुषोऽन्नरसमयः | तस्येदमेव शिरः | अयं दक्षिणः पक्षः | अयमुत्तरः पक्षः | अयमात्मा | इदं पुच्छं प्रतिष्ठा ||२.१.२||
verses.transliteration: sa vā eṣa puruṣo'nnarasamayaḥ | tasyedam eva śiraḥ | ayaṃ dakṣiṇaḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam uttaraḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam ātmā | idaṃ pucchaṃ pratiṣṭhā ||2.1.2||
verses.english_translation: This person is indeed made of the essence of food. This is his head, this his right wing, this his left wing, this his trunk, this his tail — the foundation.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नरसमयः — अन्नरसपरिणामः स्थूलशरीरम्। पञ्चकोशानां मध्ये बाह्यतमः कोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The food sheath (annamaya kosha) is the gross body, a transformation of the essence of food. It is the outermost of the five sheaths. The teaching progressively moves inward to discover the true Self beyond all sheaths.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मादन्नरसमयात् | अन्योऽन्तर आत्मा प्राणमयः | तेनैष पूर्णः ||२.२.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād annarasamayāt | anyo'ntara ātmā prāṇamayaḥ | tenaiṣa pūrṇaḥ ||2.2.1||

word_meanings.meaning: the inner Self made of prana

verses.english_translation: Different from this one made of food-essence is another inner Self made of prana. By that, this one is filled.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नरसमयात् — स्थूलशरीरात् — अन्यः — प्राणमयः आत्मा — प्राणकोशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Within the gross body (annamaya kosha) is a subtler body made of prana (pranamaya kosha). The prana-body fills and animates the food-body. This is the second of the five sheaths, progressively subtler as we move inward toward the true Self.

verses.sanskrit: स यश्चायं पुरुषे | यश्चासावादित्ये | स एकः ||२.८.५||
verses.transliteration: sa yaś cāyaṃ puruṣe | yaś cāsāv āditye | sa ekaḥ ||2.8.5||

word_meanings.meaning: that which is in the person
that which is in the sun

verses.english_translation: That which is in the person and that which is in the sun — that is one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुरुषे — आत्मा। आदित्ये — ब्रह्म। स एकः — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self in the individual person and the Brahman in the sun are one and the same. This is the teaching of identity — the microcosm and macrocosm are not different. The individual Self (jivatman) and the cosmic Self (paramatman) are one.

verses.sanskrit: स एव नमस्कर्तव्यः | स एव उपास्यः | स एव ज्ञातव्यः | स एव भवति यो एवं वेद ||२.८.५||
verses.transliteration: sa eva namaskartavyaḥ | sa eva upāsyaḥ | sa eva jñātavyaḥ | sa eva bhavati yo evaṃ veda ||2.8.5||

word_meanings.meaning: that alone is to be saluted
that alone is to be worshipped
that alone is to be known

verses.english_translation: That alone is to be saluted, that alone is to be worshipped, that alone is to be known. One who knows thus becomes that very one.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: स एव — ब्रह्म एव — नमस्कर्तव्यः — उपास्यः — ज्ञातव्यः। य एवम् वेद — स एव भवति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman alone is worthy of salutation, worship, and knowledge. One who knows Brahman thus becomes Brahman itself. This is the culminating teaching of the Taittiriya — knowledge of Brahman leads to identity with Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अहमन्नमहमन्नादोऽहमश्लोककृत् | अहमस्मि प्रथमजा ऋतस्य | पूर्वं देवेभ्योऽमृतस्य नाभाय | यो मा ददाति स इदेव मावाः ||३.१०.५||
verses.transliteration: aham annam aham annādo'ham aślokakṛt | aham asmi prathamajā ṛtasya | pūrvaṃ devebhyo'mṛtasya nābhāya | yo mā dadāti sa id eva māvāḥ ||3.10.5||

word_meanings.meaning: I am the eater of food

verses.english_translation: I am food! I am the eater of food! I am the first-born of truth, before the gods, in the navel of immortality. One who gives me — he alone saves me.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अहम् अन्नम् — अहम् ब्रह्म — अन्नरूपम्। अहम् अन्नादः — अहम् ब्रह्म — अन्नादरूपम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This ecstatic declaration of the liberated sage expresses the realization of non-duality. 'I am food' — I am the object. 'I am the eater' — I am the subject. Both subject and object are Brahman. The liberated one sees no distinction between the eater and the eaten.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽहमन्नमन्नमदन्तमद्मि | अहं विश्वं भुवनमभ्यभवाम् | सुवर्ण ज्योतीः | य एवं वेद ||३.१०.६||
verses.transliteration: so'ham annam annam adantam admi | ahaṃ viśvaṃ bhuvanam abhyabhavām | suvarṇa jyotīḥ | ya evaṃ veda ||3.10.6||

word_meanings.meaning: I eat the one who eats food
I have overcome the whole world

verses.english_translation: I eat the one who eats food! I have overcome the whole world! I am the golden light! One who knows thus.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अहम् अन्नम् अदन्तम् अद्मि — अहम् ब्रह्म — सर्वस्य भोक्ता। विश्वम् भुवनम् अभ्यभवाम् — सर्वम् अतिक्रान्तम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The liberated sage declares: 'I eat the eater' — I am the ultimate subject that consumes all objects. 'I have overcome the whole world' — I am beyond all limitation. 'I am the golden light' — I am pure consciousness. This is the triumphant declaration of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन् | सोऽश्नुते सर्वान् कामान् सह ब्रह्मणा विपश्चितेति ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo veda nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ parame vyoman | so'śnute sarvān kāmān saha brahmaṇā vipaściteti ||2.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: hidden in the cave of the heart

verses.english_translation: One who knows it hidden in the cave of the heart, in the supreme ether, attains all desires together with the all-knowing Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: गुहायाम् — हृदयाकाशे — निहितम् — ब्रह्म — वेद — सः सर्वान् कामान् अश्नुते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: One who knows Brahman hidden in the cave of the heart attains all desires — not by fulfilling individual desires but by becoming Brahman, which is the fulfillment of all desires. This is the opening and closing teaching of the Taittiriya.

verses.sanskrit: असद्वा इदमग्र आसीत् | ततो वै सदजायत | तदात्मानं स्वयमकुरुत | तस्मात्तत्सुकृतमुच्यत इति ||२.७.१||
verses.transliteration: asad vā idam agra āsīt | tato vai sad ajāyata | tad ātmānaṃ svayam akuruta | tasmāt tat sukṛtam ucyata iti ||2.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: in the beginning this was non-being

verses.english_translation: In the beginning this was non-being. From that, being arose. That made itself by itself. Therefore it is called 'well-made.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: असत् — अव्याकृतम् — अग्रे आसीत्। ततः सत् — व्याकृतम् — अजायत। आत्मानम् स्वयम् अकुरुत — स्वतः एव।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Before creation, there was the unmanifest (asat = not yet manifest). From that, the manifest (sat) arose. It made itself by itself — without any external cause or material. Therefore creation is called 'well-made' (sukritam) — it is the self-expression of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्वा एतस्मादात्मन आकाशः सम्भूतः | आकाशाद्वायुः | वायोरग्निः | अग्नेरापः | अद्भ्यः पृथिवी | पृथिव्या ओषधयः | ओषधीभ्योऽन्नम् | अन्नात्पुरुषः ||२.१.१||
verses.transliteration: tasmād vā etasmād ātmana ākāśaḥ sambhūtaḥ | ākāśād vāyuḥ | vāyor agniḥ | agner āpaḥ | adbhyaḥ pṛthivī | pṛthivyā oṣadhayaḥ | oṣadhībhyo'nnam | annāt puruṣaḥ ||2.1.1||
verses.english_translation: From this Self, space arose; from space, air; from air, fire; from fire, water; from water, earth; from earth, plants; from plants, food; from food, the person.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आत्मनः — ब्रह्मणः — आकाशः — वायुः — अग्निः — आपः — पृथिवी — ओषधयः — अन्नम् — पुरुषः — क्रमेण उत्पद्यन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The complete cosmological sequence from Brahman to the human person. From the Self (Brahman) arises space, then air, fire, water, earth, plants, food, and finally the person. The entire creation is a manifestation of Brahman — from the subtlest to the grossest.

verses.sanskrit: स वा एष पुरुषोऽन्नरसमयः | तस्येदमेव शिरः | अयं दक्षिणः पक्षः | अयमुत्तरः पक्षः | अयमात्मा | इदं पुच्छं प्रतिष्ठा | तदप्येष श्लोको भवति ||२.१.२||
verses.transliteration: sa vā eṣa puruṣo'nnarasamayaḥ | tasyedam eva śiraḥ | ayaṃ dakṣiṇaḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam uttaraḥ pakṣaḥ | ayam ātmā | idaṃ pucchaṃ pratiṣṭhā | tad apy eṣa śloko bhavati ||2.1.2||
verses.english_translation: This person is indeed made of the essence of food. This is his head, right wing, left wing, trunk, and tail — the foundation. And there is this verse about it.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अन्नरसमयः — स्थूलशरीरम् — पञ्चकोशानां मध्ये बाह्यतमः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The food sheath (annamaya kosha) is described with its five parts like a bird — head, two wings, trunk, and tail. This is the outermost of the five sheaths. The teaching progressively moves inward to discover the true Self beyond all sheaths.
